design_philosophy.core_loop: Watch briefing → Customize wanzers (mecha) with modular parts → Execute tactical mission → Return to hub for intermission setup → Progress linear story
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Strategic resource allocation and modular customization depth versus tactical positioning and target prioritization in turn-based combat
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command and customize heavily-armed walking tanks (wanzers) in geopolitical conflict scenarios; master the interplay between unit composition, part damage mechanics, and battlefield tactics
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Radical commitment to modular part-based damage system where destroying individual limbs (arms/legs) cripples functionality without eliminating units—forces tactical targeting rather than simple attrition. Deep customization layer integrated with mission design. Linear progression tied to narrative rather than exploration-driven unlocks.

difficulty.checkpointing: Mission-based checkpoint system; battles restart from intermission hub on failure
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High player agency through wanzer customization, part selection, and computer/auxiliary equipment choices. Arena feature allows optional grinding for currency and practice. Weapon class selection (melee, short-range, long-range, support fire) directly impacts tactical approach to each mission.

progression.upgrade_system: Modular wanzer customization system: body, left arm, right arm, legs parts; computers for accuracy and special abilities; auxiliary backpacks for power output and support items; four weapon classes. Player has full control within weight/power constraints.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-driven mission progression reveals new world map locations sequentially. New customization options and enemy types unlock as campaign advances. USN campaign available as alternative playthrough in remake versions.
replayability_hooks: Arena feature for repeatable AI combat and monetary rewards
Multiple campaign scenarios (USN campaign added in remakes)
Wanzer customization depth encourages experimentation with different part configurations across replays
Briefing system reveals enemy composition, enabling strategic pre-mission planning variations

world_building_techniques: Cutscene-based story progression marking major plot points
Setting in 2090 on Huffman Island establishes near-future geopolitical context
Serialized multi-character narrative following various individuals' involvement with mecha conflict
Intermission hub (towns/cities) provides narrative breathing room and unit preparation narrative justification

faction_indicators: Geopolitical conflict scenario suggests multiple military factions competing for Huffman Island control
Character-driven serialized storyline implies faction affiliation and personal motivations for protagonist and allies
Wanzer customization choices potentially reflect faction-specific equipment preferences or national military doctrine
danger_signals: Modular damage system creates tactical vulnerability states (destroyed limbs) requiring constant assessment of unit safety
Mobile weapon platforms described as 'much stronger than wanzers themselves' signal escalating threat progression
Mission variety (destroy targets vs. protect objectives) indicates changing strategic priorities and resource pressure
environmental_storytelling: World map reveals new locations sequentially, showing conflict spreading across Huffman Island geography
Battle zones become inaccessible after mission completion, suggesting permanent territorial changes and narrative progression
Intermission hub design (towns/cities as safe zones) implies civilian infrastructure threatened by ongoing warfare
